seo.title: Luma Ray 3.2: Pricing, 5s/10s Video and Examples | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Luma Ray 3.2 in MaxVideoAI for short cinematic text-to-video and image-to-video clips with 5s or 10s duration, 540p to 1080p output, prompt tips, examples and fal-reference display pricing.

overview: Luma Ray 3.2 is the current public Luma video route in MaxVideoAI for short text-to-video and image-to-video generation. The launch surface focuses on fallback-safe 5s and 10s clips at 540p, 720p or 1080p.
pricingNotes: Displayed prices use MaxVideoAI fal-reference pricing with margin applied. The exact customer-facing price appears before generation.

hero.title: Luma Ray 3.2 - current Luma cinematic video route
hero.intro: Generate short cinematic clips from a prompt or start image with public 5s and 10s controls, 540p to 1080p output and loop where the active public case supports it.

promptStructure.title: Prompt mini-guide
promptStructure.quote: Subject + action + camera move + lighting + duration + resolution + loop intent when needed.
promptStructure.description: Ray 3.2 responds best to compact shot briefs. Keep the motion, camera and output choice explicit, especially when switching from a prompt-only run to a start-image run.
steps: Name one subject and one clear action.
Add the camera move and framing.
Choose 5s for tight motion checks or 10s for slower reveals.
Use 540p or 720p for exploration and 1080p for selected checks.
Add a start image only when the first frame needs to stay anchored.
Use loop only for simple cyclical movement.

tips.title: Tips and limits
items: Keep public launch prompts inside 5s or 10s and 540p to 1080p.
Use a start image when product shape, identity or composition matters.
Use 10s when the shot needs a complete reveal; use 5s for loops and draft motion.
Avoid advertising direct-only options that are not part of the public fallback-safe surface.
Review the exact price shown in the app before generation.

prompts.title: Product reveal
prompts.prompt: Slow cinematic push-in on a premium speaker on a dark stone plinth, controlled reflections, soft rim light, subtle fabric vibration, 5s, 16:9, 720p.

prompts.title: Start image motion
prompts.prompt: Animate the uploaded product still with a gentle clockwise camera drift, keep label shape stable, soft studio reflections, 5s, 1080p.

prompts.title: 10s editorial shot
prompts.prompt: A fashion model walks through a quiet gallery at dusk, slow lateral tracking shot, warm practical lights, restrained cinematic motion, 10s, 21:9, 1080p.

prompts.title: Loop test
prompts.prompt: Minimal perfume bottle on a reflective table, tiny mist movement behind the product, perfectly stable bottle, seamless 5s loop, 1:1, 720p.

faqs.q: What does Luma Ray 3.2 support in MaxVideoAI?
faqs.a: The public model page focuses on text-to-video and image-to-video generation with 5s or 10s duration and 540p, 720p or 1080p output.

faqs.q: Does the page expose HDR or EXR options?
faqs.a: No. Public marketing copy stays on fallback-safe generation settings until any advanced direct-only options are product-approved.
Displayed examples use MaxVideoAI fal-reference pricing with margin. The exact app quote appears before generation.

faqs.q: When should I choose Ray 3.2 instead of Ray 2?
faqs.a: Choose Ray 3.2 for the current public 5s/10s generation route. Choose Ray 2 when Modify or Reframe workflows are the priority.

custom.heroSupportLine: Best for short cinematic Luma clips from prompts or start images.
custom.heroDesc1: Ray 3.2 gives teams a current Luma route for short cinematic generation while keeping public settings aligned with fallback coverage.
custom.heroDesc2: Use it for compact motion tests, start-image animation, simple loops and 1080p checks before moving clips into the rest of the production workflow.
heroHighlights: Current Luma route||Ray 3.2 is the public Luma generation route for short text or start-image clips.
Fallback-safe public surface||Marketing settings focus on 5s/10s and 540p/720p/1080p combinations covered by fal-reference pricing.
Practical loop use||Loop is best for short, simple motion rather than complex scene changes.
Clear pricing context||Displayed prices follow MaxVideoAI fal-reference pricing and the app confirms the exact quote.
custom.specNote: Specs describe the public MaxVideoAI launch surface, not unpublished direct-only controls.

specSections.title: Generation surface
specSections.intro: Ray 3.2 is exposed for short public generation routes.
items: Image-to-video when one opening still should anchor the clip.

specSections.title: Operational notes
items: Direct Luma routing may be used with fal fallback when the app cannot accept a direct request.
Fallback is not a promise of instant provider capacity.
Public pricing examples use fal-reference rates plus MaxVideoAI margin.
Advanced direct-only options are not part of this public page.

custom.whatIntro1: Start in the video workspace, choose Ray 3.2, then pick prompt-only or start-image generation.
custom.whatIntro2: Keep the shot brief specific and review the app quote before launching.
whatFlowSteps: Choose Luma Ray 3.2 as the engine.
Pick prompt-only or upload one start image.
Use loop only for simple cyclical motion.
Review the exact price and generate.
custom.galleryIntro: Use examples to compare short Luma motion patterns, start-image animation and loop-friendly prompts.
strengths: Good fit for short cinematic product, fashion, environment and social clips.
Start images help preserve composition and product shape.
1080p is useful for final inspection of selected short clips.
boundaries: Loop should be used for simple cyclical motion.
Fallback routing does not guarantee instant capacity.
safetyRules: No sexual content or content involving minors.
No hateful, harassing or graphic-violence requests.
Do not impersonate real people or public figures without consent.
Do not include private personal data or sensitive documents.
custom.promptingTitle: How to write a strong Ray 3.2 prompt
custom.promptingIntro: Write a single shot brief with subject, action, camera, lighting, duration and resolution.
custom.promptingTip: Tip: if you use a start image, describe motion and camera behavior instead of restating every visual detail.
promptingGlobalPrinciples: Use one shot goal per generation.
Keep motion physically simple for loops.
Use 10s only when the action needs the extra time.
promptingEngineWhy: Short Luma clips work best when the camera path and subject action are clear.
Start images reduce ambiguity in product and identity-led clips.
Resolution and duration choices directly affect displayed pricing.
custom.finalPara1: Ray 3.2 is the current public Luma generation route in MaxVideoAI, built for short cinematic clips with conservative, fallback-safe controls.
